rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ELIZABETH EMMA CAULFIELD MAYO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/28/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A proof of payment of assessment must be filed. 2) The original bank letter for the non-exempt trust account holding the conservatee's funds has not been filed. 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The filing date and document title must be identified for the $495 cost reimbursement to Hall Law Firm on 04/01/2018. B) A schedule of RMDs made during this account period must be provided. C) Has the petitioner obtained an order from the Court pursuant to Probate Code section 2459(c) to withdraw RMDs from the IRA account? D) Should a separate petition for change of venue be filed after the conservatee has actually moved to San Mateo County?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ERNESTO JAUREGUI SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/20. No CIU report yet. No courtesy copy of the late filed supplement has been received by the Examiner as of 8/26/20. Schedule E of the account does not show the market value of the assets on hand as of 12/31/19. Fiduciary fees calculated on the FMV of assets includes the preparation of the account; payment to a third party to prepare the court accounting must be deducted from the trustee fee. "Court filing fee" should show what was filed and itemized to show any cost paid to a vendor. Reimbursements to anyone should indicate who the original payee was. "Discretionary Payment for Beneficiary" should have an actual, fuller description (Who uses the phone? What were the entertainment expenses for?). A bank trustee does not need to file bank statements. [LRSF 14.62.] Attorney costs described simply as "File & Serve" are not sufficient; the billing entry should show what was filed (and what the separate costs to the vendor were). Why are full copies of the petition and the order sent to 10 recipients?

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/28/2020 (no earlier date available) due to the amended petition requiring new notice. CRC 7.53(a). 1) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Paragraph 3: Is the correct expiration date 08/12/2019? B) Paragraph 9: Did the petitioner give notice of proposed action to the requestor of special notice? Has the requestor consented to the sale? See Probate Code sections 10501(c)(3) & 10581(c). C) Paragraph 10(c): Section 1064(a)(3) requires a statement of compensation paid "other than" pursuant to a prior court order. 2) No proposed orders complying with LRSF 14.20(C) have been provided. 3) A copy of a statement of each account listed on Schedule I showing the amended ending date value must be filed with a declaration regarding the retention of original statements. LRSF 14.93D. 4) A proof of service showing that a copy of the supplement was given to the requestor of special notice must be filed. CRC 7.53(b).

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Both counsel and guardian must appear. Issues may include (but not limited to): Failure to file an inventory and appraisal. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANDREW R. DOEDEN MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/05/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the civil court's order approving the minor's compromise is not on file. 2) The petitioner has not been appointed as guardian ad litem for the minor in this trust proceeding. 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The minor's age, health, and living situation must be described. B) What is the petitioner's relationship with the minor? C) The trust instrument must prohibit modification or revocation without court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(2). D) Article 3, Section 1, of the trust instrument: Are discretionary distributions subject to approval by the Court when accountings are reviewed? E) Article 5, Section 1a, of the trust instrument: Why is the petitioner proposing a limited power of appointment (and not a general power of appointment)? F) Article 5, Section 1b, of the trust instrument: Who are the minor's heirs? G) Article 6, Section 1, of the trust instrument: Trustee resignation must require court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(7). H) Article 6, Section 3, of the trust instrument: Appointment of James Doeden as successor trustee requires court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(7). I) Article 6, Section 5, of the trust instrument: Is delegation of discretionary powers appropriate? J) Article 6, Section 7, of the trust instrument: Is a hold harmless clause appropriate? K) Article 7, Section 6, of the trust instrument: Compensation (including fees on account) must require prior court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(8). L) Article 8, Section 3(f), of the trust instrument: The cited statute regarding a common trust fund is incorrect. M) All the factors set forth in Probate Code section 2574(b) must be addressed for the proposed investments in mutual funds and long-term government bonds. Also, the proposed portfolio must be described in more details including managers and fees. N) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the trust must be provided. LSRF 14.79(B).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: as noted in the prior tentative ruling, no copy of the petition, no proposed order have been provided, and there is no POS of the NOH form on file. as none of these issues was addressed by the 8/24/20 set by the examiner, the petition is continued to 11/16/20. all issues must be resolved by noon on 11/9/20 or an appearance will be required to determine whether the petition should be taken off calendar.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether a spousal property petition is separately required to confirm Freddie Cotton's succession of the decedent's interest in the subject real property. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
